summary: Ukrainian forces report multiple hostile UAV incursions across several oblasts, including reactive drones targeting Poltava and Sumy. Russian-backed threats of Iskander-M/S-300 ballistic missile launches from Belgorod and occupied Crimea are noted. Civilian infrastructure suffered damage, such as the complete destruction of the Chongar bridge in Crimea.
key_points: Numerous reactive and reconnaissance UAVs were observed over Sumy, Kharkiv, Dnipropetrovsk, Poltava and other regions, many heading toward strategic targets (Source: @kpszsu/64827).
A ballistic missile threat was reported from Belgorod region, potentially involving Iskander-M or S-300 systems (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67546).
The Chongar bridge in occupied Crimea was reported completely destroyed (Source: @UkraineNow/81821).
Iranian drones struck US Fifth Fleet assets in Bahrain (Source: @tass_agency/380166).
Ukrainian officials announced acquisition talks for additional air defence missiles, including PAC-2 and PAC-3 (Source: @UkraineNow/81828).
A Russian SBU agent planning an assassination of a Russian Ministry of Defence employee was detained (Source: @tass_agency/380200).
Ukrainian forces claimed destruction of Mariupol port infrastructure and a Russian shadow fleet vessel (Source: @UkraineNow/81817).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian forces report a surge of hostile UAV activity across multiple front‑line oblasts, with reactive drones targeting Poltava and Sumy (Source: @kpszsu/64827). Simultaneously, Russian‑backed threats of Iskander‑M/S‑300 ballistic missile launches from Belgorod and occupied Crimea raise strategic alarm (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67546). Civilian and logistical infrastructure continues to suffer, exemplified by the total destruction of the Chongar bridge in Crimea (Source: @UkraineNow/81821).

points: Ukrainian troops reported a reactive UAV from Sumy heading toward Poltava, specifically targeting Kotelva/Opishnya (Source: @kpszsu/64827).
A reconnaissance UAV was observed over the Kharkiv region near Bogoduhiv (Source: @eRadarrua/96163).
Ukrainian forces claimed to have destroyed Mariupol port infrastructure, including power stations, radar, and the shadow fleet vessel "Lady Augusta" (Source: @UkraineNow/81817).
The Ukrainian Ministry of Foreign Affairs announced negotiations to obtain additional air‑defence missiles such as PAC‑2 and PAC‑3 (Source: @UkraineNow/81828).
Ukrainian authorities reported the complete demolition of the Chongar bridge in occupied Crimea (Source: @UkraineNow/81821).
Iranian drones struck US Fifth Fleet forces in Bahrain, an event reported by Iranian state TV (Source: @tass_agency/380166).
A Russian SBU agent planning the murder of a Russian Ministry of Defence employee was arrested (Source: @tass_agency/380200).
Russian authorities noted a fire in a Sevastopol building after a Ukrainian attack (Source: @tass_agency/380188).
Russian officials reported a bus driver incident in Yekaterinburg, unrelated to combat (Source: @tass_agency/380197).
The Russian Ministry of Energy reported external power loss at Zaporizhzhia NPP, with diesel generators running (Source: @tass_agency/380199).
Reactive UAVs were sighted over Sumy moving toward the north‑eastern direction of Chernihiv region (Source: @kpszsu/64832).
A reconnaissance UAV "Zala" operated near Krolevets in Sumy oblast (Source: @eRadarrua/96144).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV over Poltava heading toward the city (Source: @eRadarrua/96197).
Multiple UAVs were observed near the Black Sea coast heading toward Ochakiv and Chornomorsk (Source: @kpszsu/64834).
A UAV was detected over the Dnipropetrovsk region heading toward Magdalyne (Source: @kpszsu/64814).
UAV activity reported near Zaporizhzhia heading north‑west (Source: @kpszsu/64839).
UAVs observed over Sumy moving toward Chernihiv (Source: @kpszsu/64832).
A reactive UAV from Sumy was seen heading toward Glukhov, southwest course (Source: @kpszsu/64838).
UAVs detected over Kharkiv near Kolomak moving toward Poltava direction (Source: @kpszsu/64835).
UAVs spotted over Dnipropetrovsk heading toward Ternevka, Pavlograd (Source: @kpszsu/64828).
A ballistic missile threat over Poltava was reported, with mentions of "ballistic missile" (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67548).
A Shahed‑type reactive drone was reported over Poltava (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67553).
Threat of Iskander‑M launches from occupied Crimea was noted (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67559).
Ukrainian forces claimed destruction of Mariupol port facilities, affecting Russian logistics (Source: @UkraineNow/81817).
Ukrainian officials reported a missile strike on the Ukrainian postal hub, causing fire and parcel loss (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30822).
Ukrainian sources reported limited UAV presence in border regions, stating only a few remain (Source: @eRadarrua/96186).
Russian tactical aviation activity was flagged as a warning (Source: @kpszsu/64840).
Iranian drones engaged US naval forces in Bahrain (Source: @tass_agency/380166).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV over the Black Sea near Tuzly (Source: @eRadarrua/96143).
A UAV was observed over the Black Sea heading toward the Zatoka area (Source: @eRadarrua/96164).

key_developments: Reactive UAVs from Sumy moved toward Poltava, targeting Kotelva/Opishnya (Source: @kpszsu/64827).
A ballistic missile threat involving Iskander‑M or S‑300 systems was reported from Belgorod region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67546).
The Chongar bridge in occupied Crimea was completely destroyed (Source: @UkraineNow/81821).
Iranian drones struck US Fifth Fleet assets in Bahrain (Source: @tass_agency/380166).
Ukrainian forces claimed destruction of Mariupol port infrastructure and a Russian shadow‑fleet vessel (Source: @UkraineNow/81817).
Ukraine announced negotiations to acquire additional air‑defence missiles, including PAC‑2 and PAC‑3 (Source: @UkraineNow/81828).
A Russian SBU agent planning an assassination of a Ministry of Defence employee was detained (Source: @tass_agency/380200).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian territory continues to face a high volume of hostile UAV incursions, especially reactive drones targeting key logistics hubs in Poltava and Sumy. The reported possibility of Iskander‑M or S‑300 ballistic missile launches from Belgorod and occupied Crimea elevates the strategic threat level, potentially enabling long‑range strikes. Destruction of critical infrastructure such as the Chongar bridge hampers Russian mobility in Crimea, while Iranian drone attacks on US naval assets demonstrate the expanding regional dimension of the conflict.
sections.regional_response: Western partners are engaged in negotiations to supply Ukraine with additional air‑defence systems, including PAC‑2 and PAC‑3 missiles, indicating increased support for counter‑UAV and missile defence. Russian authorities have highlighted internal security incidents, such as the arrest of an SBU agent, reflecting heightened counter‑intelligence measures. International actors, notably the United States, have responded to Iranian drone activity with additional strikes, showing a broader coalition response to regional escalations.
sections.intelligence_notes: UAV tracking data shows a pattern of east‑west movement from Sumy and Kharkiv toward central Ukrainian regions, suggesting a focus on disrupting supply lines. The ballistic missile threat from Belgorod and Crimea aligns with Russian attempts to re‑establish long‑range strike capabilities. Infrastructure attacks, including the Chongar bridge demolition, indicate a Russian strategy to degrade Ukrainian logistical routes while simultaneously protecting key assets from further UAV attacks.
